name: Onion & Bacon Tart - Flammekueche
recipeInstructions:
-------------
Top Topping:
12 bacon slices, cut into 1/2-inch pieces (about 7 oz)
4 medium Onions, thinly sliced
4 cups Small curd cottage cheese
1/4 cup + 1 tablespoon Creme fraiche or whipping cream
1 tablespoon Vegetable oil
1/2 teaspoon Salt
1/4 teaspoon Ground nutmeg
1/4 teaspoon Ground pepper
For Dough:
1. Mix flour, 3/4 cup water, salt, and oil in a large bowl.
Add more water by tablespoons if the dough is very dry.
2. Turn out dough onto lightly floured surface and knead until smooth and elastic, about 3 minutes.
3. Wrap in plastic and refrigerate overnight. Bring to room temperature before continuing.
For Topping:
1. Preheat oven to 500°F.
2. Saute bacon in a heavy large skillet over medium-low heat until brown, about 10 minutes.
Using a slotted spoon, transfer bacon to paper towels; drain.
3. Add onions to the skillet with bacon fat and saute over medium heat until tender and light brown, about 10 minutes.
4. Remove from heat. Puree cottage cheese in a blender until almost smooth.
5. Transfer to a large bowl. Add creme fraiche, oil, salt, nutmeg, and pepper; stir to blend.
6. Divide dough into 4 equal pieces. From each piece, form balls; flatten into disks.
7. On lightly floured surface, roll out 1 disk to a 12-inch round. Place the round on a pizza pan or baking sheet.
8. Repeat with remaining dough pieces, placing each round on separate pizza pans or baking sheets.
9. Divide cream mixture among rounds, spreading to within 1 inch of the edge.
10. Top each with bacon and onions.
11. Working in batches, bake pizzas until the cream bubbles and begins to brown, about 12 minutes.
Bon Appetit/May 94
